content.problemStatement:
Chef comes across a very interesting problem.

He has an array AAA of length NNN, and his task is to **delete** some elements from the array to make the array good.

An array is termed good if:


The length of the array is **even**;
Let the length of the array AAA after some (possibly zero) deletions be MMM, where MMM is even.

Then, A1+A2=A3+A4=…=AM−1+AMA_1+A_2 = A_3 + A_4 = \ldots = A_{M-1} + A_MA1​+A2​=A3​+A4​=…=AM−1​+AM​ should hold true.
Help Chef find the **minimum** number of deletions to make the array good.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers representing the initial elements in the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum number of deletions Chef has to perform in order to make the array good.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given array has length 666 which is even, but A1+A2=3A_1 + A_2 = 3A1​+A2​=3 is not equal to A3+A4=2A_3+A_4 = 2A3​+A4​=2.
To make the array good, Chef can delete elements at indices 333 and 666.
Then, we are left with array [1,2,1,2][1,2,1,2][1,2,1,2]. Here, A1+A2=A3+A4=3A_1 + A_2 = A_3 + A_4 = 3A1​+A2​=A3​+A4​=3.
It can be shown that we cannot make the array good in less than 222 deletions.
Test case 222: The given array has length 666 and A1+A2=A3+A4=A5+A6=2A_1 + A_2 = A_3 + A_4 = A_5 + A_6 = 2A1​+A2​=A3​+A4​=A5​+A6​=2.
Thus, the array is good and we do not require any operations.
